content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 19, 1987

George Holiday
11402 Downey Avenue
Downey, CA 90241
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-87-238

Dear Mr. Holiday:

You have requested advice concerning your disclosure
requirements under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Are you required to disclose investments or interests in real
property which you held prior to the date when the project area
committee, on which you serve as chair, was added to the City of
Downey's conflict of interest code?

CONCLUSION

Members of project area committees must disclose reportable
investments and interests in real property which are held on the
date they become designated in their city's conflict of interest
code.

FACTS

You are the chair of the City of Downey's Redevelopment
Project Area Committee ("PAC"). The City revised its conflict of
interest code on July 28, 1987, to require that members of the
Redevelopment PAC file statements of economic interests ("SEI").
Downey Redevelopment PAC members were not required to file SEIs
previously.

_________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

George Holiday
October 19, 1987
Page 2

ANALYSIS

The Commission has ruled recently that members of PACs must be
included as designated employees in a city's conflict of interest
code. (In re Rotman (1987) 10 FPPC Ops 1, copy enclosed.) The
City of Downey revised its conflict of interest code on July 28,
1987, to require members of the Redevelopment PAC to file SEIs.
The Commission made it clear in Rotman that;
"...those who were
not designated in a code, have not been subject to the
requirements to file disclosure statements, need not file
statements for past years." (In re Rotman, Supra at p. 9.)

You should complete an "initial" statement for designated
employees, Form 730. Such a statement requires disclosure of your
reportable investments and real property interests held on the
effective date of the amended conflict of interest code adopted by
the City of Downey.

If you have any questions regarding the advice in this letter,
please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By:

Bruce W. Robeck
Political Reform Consultant

DMG:BWR:kmt

GEORGE HOLIDAY

11402 Downey Avenue

Downey, California, 90241

(213) 862-2345


September 15, 1987

Mr. Bruce Robeck
Technical Assistance Department
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street, P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Robeck:

Please accept my thanks for your assistance in clarifying two
important points for me yesterday in my request for assistance in
filing Form 730 as Chairman of the Downey Redevelopment Project
Area Committee.

It was most helpful to have your ruling that the Reporting Period
in this instance starts when the City's Resolution adopting the
Conflict of Interest Code was adopted on July 28, 1987 and that
there is no retrospective reporting requirement.

I especially appreciate your courtesy in phoning me later in the
day to affirm that your legal department confirmed your position;
I am never quite comfortable when an important issue is at hand
and the other party says, "If you don't hear back from me,
everything is O.K." So, thank you again for putting me at ease.

With this thought in mind, and since this is an important matter,
I would appreciate it greatly if you would send me a confirming
letter for my file.

Sincerely,

George Holiday

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 21, 1987


George Holiday
11402 Downey Avenue
Downey, CA 90241

Re: 87-238

Dear Mr. Holiday:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on September 18, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[illegible]

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division
